opportunities.title: Audubon Park Garden District Clean-Up Fall 2026
opportunities.description: Audubon Park Garden District Clean-Up Fall 2026 Keep Orlando Beautiful 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Join Keep Orlando Beautiful and the Audubon Park Garden District for a community cleanup! Saturday, September 26 9:00 - 11:00 a.m. Meet in front of Redlight Redlight to check in and grab supplies. Volunteers will help enhance the community by picking up litter throughout Audubon Park. All necessary supplies including vests, grabbers, gloves, and trash bags will be provided. Please wear comfortable clothing and close-toed shoes. Bring a refillable water bottle, hat, sunscreen, etc. We hope to see you there! Additional info: Location Where the opportunity will occur 2810 Corrine Dr. Orlando , FL 32803 Location Description: Redlight Redlight Get Directions Coordinates: 28.567509,-81.347685 Contact Info Contact person for the opportunity Contact 1 Email: keeporlandobeautiful@orlando.gov Primary Phone: (407) 246-2752 Contact 2 Contact Address Address to use when contacting someone about this opportunity. opportunities.title: Weed n' Mulch at South Street Urban Farm 07/01/2026
opportunities.description: Weed n' Mulch at South Street Urban Farm 07/01/2026 Office of Sustainability, Resilience and Future Ready 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Date: Wednesday, July 1, 2026 Location: 626 W South St. Orlando, FL 32805 Time: 8:00 am - 10:00 am Parking: Parking will be available on the street next to the garden and in the grass lot across the street, located at 617 W South Street Orlando, FL 32805. Weekly summer maintenance at South Street Urban Farm, including weeding, mulching, planting, and garden management. Please bring water, sunscreen and closed toe shoes! Bathrooms are not available on-site, so please plan accordingly. --------------------------------------------- The Office of Sustainability, Resilience and Future Ready is thrilled to offer Volunteer Workdays at South Street Urban Farm! South Street Urban Farm is a hybrid educational urban farm and community-supported garden, which seeks to meet the need for secure, fresh, and culturally relevant food, and provides opportunities for learning about regenerative gardening. *Please note that unfortunately, the coordinator of this activity is unable to sign off on court-mandated community service hours. Additional info: Minimum age: 18 Location Where the opportunity will occur 626 W South Street Orlando , FL 32805 Location Description: Get Directions Coordinates: 28.538018,-81.38773 Contact Info Contact person for the opportunity Contact 1 Contact Name: Vern Renzette Primary Phone: (561) 279-5692 Contac

opportunities.title: Plastic Free July Cleanup
opportunities.description: Plastic Free July Cleanup Keep Orlando Beautiful 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Join Keep Orlando Beautiful for a community cleanup! All month long we are celebrating Plastic Free July. This means we are ditching the single-use plastics and choosing reusables! This cleanup we will be focusing on removing these single-use plastics like water bottles, utensils, and straws from our beautiful wild spaces. Eagle Nest Park Saturday, July 18 8:00 - 10:00 a.m. Please dress for a mess as volunteers will be working outdoors. Please wear close-toed shoes and bring other items such as sun-screen, bug spray, a hat or other items as needed. Make sure to bring your reusbale water bottle and help us go plastic free! We will have a water cooler with a spout for refills. If you forget your water bottle water fountains are also available. We hope to see you at the event! Additional info: Location Where the opportunity will occur 5165 Metrowest Blvd Orlando , FL 32811 Location Description: Sign in at the tables near the restroom. Get Directions Coordinates: 28.5171,-81.449721 Contact Info Contact person for the opportunity Contact 1 Email: keeporlandobeautiful@orlando.gov Primary Phone: (407) 246-2752 Contact 2 Contact Address Address to use when contacting someone about this opportunity. opportunities.title: Strawless Summer Restaurant Crawl
opportunities.description: Strawless Summer Restaurant Crawl Keep Orlando Beautiful 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Strawless Summer is an Orlando-wide initiative led by the Surfrider Orlando Chapter and Keep Orlando Beautiful to help local restaurants reduce single-use plastic waste! For 2 months (July through August 2026), participating businesses pledge to only provide straws upon customer request. This simple shift helps: Cut down on unnecessary single-use waste Save restaurants money on straw costs Prevent straw debris from littering Orlando We need volunteers to help us invite restaurants to join us! Monday June 22 - Milk District Wednesday June 24 - Ivanhoe Village Thursday June 25 - Mills 50 Check volunteer shift for exact locations. Volunteer Activities: Join the crawl and break into small groups. Each group will visit several restaurants sharing the Strawless Summer flyer. Help restaurants sign up online. Pass out buttons and signage if they sign up! Join us in making a positive impact and support a cleaner Orlando! Additional info: Minimum age: 16 Availability Schedule Shifts Opportunity available: 06/04/2026 through Unlimited Openings There are no available shifts for this opportunity. 3 Schedule Shift available! 3 Schedule Shifts available! opportunities.title: Upcycled T-Shirt Bag Sewing Workshop 2026
opportunities.description: Upcycled T-Shirt Bag Sewing Workshop 2026 Keep Orlando Beautiful 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description We are looking for volunteers to help us at our sewing workshops at the Orlando Science Center. We will be helping teach the kids how to use a sewing machine and make upcycled t-shirt bags in their sewing lab! Workshops will take place on Mondays in July 10:30 a.m. to 1:30 p.m. Volunteer Requirements: Comfortable using a sewing machine. Volunteers 16+ with parent signature. We can sign off on school volunteer hours at the event if needed. Court mandated community service hours will not be honored for this shift. Park at the Orlando Science Center's parking garage and we will provide parking validation. The event will be in the Hive. Just let the staff up front know you are volunteering with Keep Orlando Beautiful and they can let you in. opportunities.title: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Visitor Center
opportunities.description: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Visitor Center Orlando Wetlands 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Join our community of friendly & dedicated volunteers, the Friends of the Orlando Wetlands, to support the mission and operations of the Orlando Wetlands. Become an Orlando Wetlands ambassador today! BEFORE SIGNING UP: Attending an orientation session is required. Upcoming 2026 options: Sat, July 25th Sat, September 19th Sat, November 14th 8:30 AM - 11:30 AM. Times/dates subject to change. LIMITED SPOTS, RSVP REQUIRED. Activities include: Front Desk: Tues - Sat General operation of the Visitor Center, including opening/closing procedures, greet visitors, provide info, phone reception, restock, light cleaning/organization, process transactions, operate basic tech, & other duties as needed/assigned. Docent: Tues - Sat Greet visitors, interpret displays, live animals & models, lead hands-on interactive activities, run the Education Cart, operate basic tech, interact with all ages, stand/sit for long periods of time, & other duties as needed/assigned. Time Commitment: Volunteers participate on an ongoing & recurring basis such as weekly or monthly. Shifts ~ 4 hours in length; 8:30 AM - 12:30 PM (morning) or 12:15 PM - 4:30 PM (afternoon). Setting: Most work is indoors/directly with people, but will occasionally be in a wetlands environment, potentially exposed to weather/wildlife. Work will involve interacting with the public, providing info, standing/sitting, etc

opportunities.title: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Tram Tours or Gardening
opportunities.description: Volunteer: Orlando Wetlands Tram Tours or Gardening Orlando Wetlands 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Join the Friends of the Orlando Wetlands volunteer group to support the mission and operations of the Orlando Wetlands. Activities include: Wednesday mornings Maintain wildlife garden and other planting projects in the Wetlands. Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays all day Conducting guided tours and tram tour logistics: recording golf cart tour data, processing donations, managing parking (if needed), etc. Other Periodically, there are other volunteer opportunities to assist with at outreach events, conduct tours for reserved groups, and more. Time Commitment: Volunteers participate on an ongoing and recurring basis such as weekly or monthly. Shift hours vary, but are typically no less than 4 hours in length. Setting: Most work is conducted outdoors in a wetlands environment where volunteers will be exposed to weather, wildlife, people. Work may involve operating tram carts and using basic gardening tools. Skills: No qualifications required. Benefits: Being a friend of the "Friends of the Orlando Wetlands" group! Opportunities to participate in shaping the direction of our programs and volunteer opportunities. Periodic social and learning events. Training: Training will be provided by staff and experienced volunteers. All new volunteers wanting to lead tours will shadow staff and other volunteers on tours until they are ready to lead on their own. Screeni

opportunities.title: Volunteer Open House for Harry P. Leu Gardens - July 2026
opportunities.description: Volunteer Open House for Harry P. Leu Gardens - July 2026 Harry P. Leu Gardens 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Harry P. Leu Gardens Volunteer Open House: July 11th, 10:00am to 11:30am Join a vibrant community of nature lovers, history enthusiasts, and service-minded individuals who help make Harry P. Leu Gardens a beautiful and welcoming place for all. From greeting guests and leading tours to helping in the gardens and supporting special events, our volunteers play a vital role in everything we do. Whether you have a few hours a week or are looking for seasonal opportunities, there's a place for you to grow, give back, and connect. Our Volunteer Open House is the start of your volunteering journey! We will cover all the different types of volunteer opportunities available including horticultural, educational, and event support. After the Open House, you will have the option of which team (or teams!) to join and will be given access to all our specific volunteer opportunities. Schedule: 10:00am - Meet & greet staff and volunteers 10:15am - Official start and introduction 10:30am - Department breakdowns 10:50am - Orland Cares Tutorial 11:00am - Questions We will end with an optional tour of the Gardens! Additional info: Minimum age: 18 Availability Schedule Shifts Opportunity available: 07/11/2026 through 22 Positions available! There are no available shifts for this opportunity. Schedule Shift available! Schedule Shifts available! Show hide schedule

opportunities.title: CERT Volunteers - Community Emergency Response Team
opportunities.description: CERT Volunteers - Community Emergency Response Team Orlando Fire Department 0 similar opportunities Similar Opportunities Description Position: The Community Emergency Response Team (CERT) program educates volunteers about disaster preparedness for the hazards that may occur where they live. CERT trains volunteers in basic disaster response skills, such as: Fire safety Light search and rescue Team organization Disaster medical operations The CERT program offers a consistent, nationwide approach to volunteer training and organization that professional responders can rely on during disaster situations, allowing them to focus on more complex tasks. Qualifications: Volunteers must complete a mandatory CERT Basic Training either online or in-person in order to be CERT certified. Volunteers will also need to complete and pass a background screening prior to beginning their service. Responsibilities: Once CERT trained, volunteers will have the opportunity to participate in community events focused on safety and disaster preparedness. Additional info: Minimum age: 16 Availability Schedule Shifts Opportunity available: 06/29/2023 through 206 Positions available! There are no available shifts for this opportunity. Schedule Shift available! Schedule Shifts available!
